# Brief: D1 — provider union + arktype widening + consumer adaptation

## Task

In `packages/1-framework/1-core/config/src/contract-source-types.ts`, split
`ContractSourceProvider` into a `sourceFormat`-keyed union of three exported members —
`PslContractSourceProvider` (`sourceFormat: 'psl'`), `TypeScriptContractSourceProvider`
(`sourceFormat: 'typescript'`), `OpaqueContractSourceProvider` (open
`sourceFormat?: string`) — each carrying the existing shared shape (`inputs?`, `load`).
`ContractSourceProvider` becomes the union of the three. `ContractSourceFormat` stops
being a closed `'psl' | 'typescript'` enum at the provider boundary (unknown strings are
admitted as opaque). Widen the arktype schema in
`packages/1-framework/1-core/config/src/config-types.ts` (`'sourceFormat?'` from
`"'psl' | 'typescript'"` to `'string'`); no new required keys; undeclared keys on the
provider object must remain ignored by validation. Adapt every in-repo consumer that
typecheck surfaces. Tests first (union assignability, schema admission, fixture
stability), then the type change.

## Scope

**In:**

- `packages/1-framework/1-core/config/src/{contract-source-types,config-types}.ts` + the
  config package's tests (`test/config-types.test-d.ts` and siblings).
- Type-level adaptation of in-repo consumers surfaced by `pnpm typecheck` — expected
  candidates: `packages/2-sql/2-authoring/contract-ts/src/config-types.ts`,
  `packages/2-mongo-family/2-authoring/contract-ts/src/config-types.ts`, the two
  `contract-psl` providers' return types,
  `packages/1-framework/3-tooling/language-server/src/schema-inputs.ts`,
  `packages/1-framework/3-tooling/cli/src/{commands,control-api/operations}/format.ts`.
  These sites compare `sourceFormat` for equality (don't rely on narrowing); keep their
  behavior identical for unknown format strings (treat as opaque — the documented
  behavior for absent `sourceFormat`).

**Out:**

- No `interpret` anywhere — no capability type, no guard (that is D2).
- No changes to `psl-parser`.
- No provider behavior changes; `load` signatures untouched.
- No changes under `packages/3-targets/**`, `docs/`, `projects/`.

## Completed when

- [ ] `ContractSourceProvider` is the three-member union; the three member types are
      exported from the same module; existing provider literals
      (`sourceFormat: 'psl'` / `'typescript'` / absent) remain assignable without casts.
- [ ] Config-package tests prove: (a) a provider object with `sourceFormat: 'made-up-format'`
      validates; (b) a provider object carrying an extra `interpret` key validates
      untouched; (c) existing sql + mongo config fixtures validate unchanged.
- [ ] Full gate green (see Validation gates); zero new `blindCast`/`castAs`; no
      cast-ratchet movement.

## Standing instruction

Stay focused on the goal; control scope. Trivial-and-related fixes that obviously serve
the goal go in the same dispatch with a one-line note in your wrap-up message. Anything
that pulls you off the goal — even if it looks useful — halts and surfaces.

## References

- Slice spec: `projects/lsp-interpreter-diagnostics/slices/01-provider-union-capability-seam/spec.md` — design + SDoD + pre-investigated edge cases.
- Slice plan: same file, § Dispatch plan § D1.
- Project spec (background): `projects/lsp-interpreter-diagnostics/spec.md` — §§ At a glance, Contract impact, Transitional-shape constraints.
- Repo conventions: `AGENTS.md` golden rules (tests before implementation; arktype not zod; no bare `as`; build with `pnpm`).

## Operational metadata

- **Model tier:** harness default (Zed sub-agent) — type-mechanical change with a clear spec.
- **Time-box:** 60 min wall-clock. Overrun → halt and surface, do not extend.
- **Halt conditions:** an out-of-scope surface needs *behavioral* (not type-level) changes
  to compile; the arktype widening breaks an existing fixture in a way that suggests the
  union shape is wrong; diff exceeds ~15 files; any consumer genuinely relies on
  narrowing via `sourceFormat === 'psl'` (i.e. accesses member-specific properties after
  the check) — that contradicts a slice-spec edge-case assumption and must surface, not
  be worked around.
